symptoms.symptom_name: Localised throbbing pain in a lower right molar, worse on biting/percussion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal tooth pain worse on biting localises the source to a single tooth's periapical tissues.
Constant throbbing pain with a focal tender tooth is unlike the brief paroxysms of trigeminal neuralgia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fluctuant gingival swelling with purulent exudate at the gum margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised fluctuant gingival collection discharging pus adjacent to the culprit tooth is characteristic of a dental abscess; visible pus is patient-observable but true fluctuance requires examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender, slightly mobile molar on percussion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Percussion tenderness and mobility indicate periapical/periodontal breakdown around the tooth — a clinician examination finding, not remotely obtainable.
Spread of infection beyond the periapical space into facial soft tissue.
Submandibular extension is the anatomical pathway to deep-space involvement and airway threat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Trismus — mouth opening reduced to ~2 cm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Trismus signals inflammation of the muscles of mastication from deep-space spread — a marker of a higher-risk infection warranting escalation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spreading odontogenic infection with systemic involvement
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic features indicate the infection has moved beyond a localised abscess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vitals — Temp 38.3 degrees C, HR 102, BP 135/85, RR 18, SpO2 99%

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spreading odontogenic infection with systemic involvement
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever and tachycardia are objective systemic markers — clinician-measured; the AI Doctor infers systemic upset from reported fever/malaise, not from measured numbers.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthopantomogram showing periapical radiolucency at the culprit molar

investigation_details.expected_result: Periapical radiolucency indicating apical bone loss around the affected molar
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Source tooth not confirmed for definitive dental treatment; does not change the immediate need to escalate.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic periapical bone loss confirms the odontogenic source — a hospital/dental investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Airway currently intact — swallowing saliva, normal voice, no stridor, no floor-of-mouth swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A currently patent airway makes established Ludwig's angina less likely NOW, but does not remove the risk given trismus and rapid spread — it defines the escalation trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Supratherapeutic OTC analgesic use (ibuprofen and paracetamol above recommended limits)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exceeding OTC limits, with reduced oral intake and dehydration, raises the risk of GI bleeding, acute kidney injury and hepatotoxicity.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Deep-space odontogenic infection with airway-threat potential
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: spreading odontogenic infection / Ludwig's angina risk
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Submandibular swelling + trismus + systemic features together lift this from a simple abscess to a deep-space infection requiring urgent face-to-face escalation and airway vigilance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent face-to-face airway and floor-of-mouth assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The trismus + rapid submandibular spread cannot be safely assessed remotely; the airway must be evaluated in person.
Identifies the culprit tooth and confirms the periapical source for definitive dental treatment — arranged in the ED/dental setting, not a reason to delay escalation.
Assess the degree of systemic inflammatory response / sepsis in the emergency setting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT neck with contrast (if deep-space infection suspected in ED)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the extent of any deep neck-space collection to guide surgical drainage — a hospital decision.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient dental review only (without escalation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deferring to a routine dental appointment under-triages a spreading infection with trismus and systemic features.
